top_signals.title: EU Says No Jet Fuel Shortage Coming Despite Middle East Supply Loss
top_signals.summary: Shortages of jet fuel are not looming over Europe despite the shock loss of supply from the Middle East and soaring prices, according to EU Sustainable Transport and Tourism Commissioner, Apostolos Tzitzikostas. “There is currently no jet fuel shortage in Europe. We have no signs that we will have a shortage in the coming period,” Tzitzikostas told Reuters in an interview published on Friday. Since the war in Iran broke out on February 28, European airlines have been struggling with a spike in jet fuel prices, while officials and analysts…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Oman Oil Terminal Attack Rattles the Market's Last Calm Corner
top_signals.summary: Media reports about a blast disrupting oil loadings at Oman’s main terminal pushed benchmark prices higher earlier today, in the latest sign that any hopes about an end to Persian Gulf hostilities is probably premature. At the time of writing, Brent crude was trading at $95.37 per barrel, and West Texas Intermediate was changing hands for $93.04 per barrel on the futures market, modestly up on Thursday, when prices dropped on reports about a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. Later news coverage, however, revealed that Hezbollah has…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Warren Buffett disciple Guy Spier built career on value investing. A rare cancer changed everything
top_signals.summary: After a glioblastoma diagnosis, value investor Guy Spier rethought wealth, time and legacy — and the impact he can have on rare disease research.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

Ether.fi allocates $100 million to a Plume RWA vault, giving users access to yield

top_signals.title: Supreme Court rules FCC fines punishing telecom giants for sharing location data were legal
top_signals.summary: The Trump administration had backed the FCC’s position and, apart from Justice Clarence Thomas, the high court agreed.
